description:
## Goal

Two related changes to the FzValue tag scheme:

1. **Swap TAG_INT and TAG_PTR.** Make TAG_INT = 0b000 so that an integer's representation is `n << 3` and addition/subtraction of two tagged integers produces a correctly-tagged result with **no untagging**. TAG_PTR moves to 0b001; pointer dereference costs one extra `and ~7` per access. Integer arithmetic vastly outnumbers pointer dereferences in typical code, so this is a clear win.
2. **Remove TAG_SPECIAL.** nil, true, and false become reserved atoms (atom 0 = nil, atom 1 = true, atom 2 = false). Single-u64 equality checks against the new constants cost the same as before; the `Tag::Special` enum variant and three FzValue constructors collapse into `from_atom`.

Net result: a cleaner four-entry tag table with four unused slots reserved for future tag types (TAG_LIST, TAG_FWD, TAG_FLOAT, etc.).

## Final tag layout

```
0b000 = TAG_INT     (n << 3; free add/sub; mul/div still need one shift)
0b001 = TAG_PTR     (ptr | 1; deref costs one `and ~7`)
0b010 = TAG_ATOM    (id << 3 | 2; nil = atom 0, true = atom 1, false = atom 2)
0b011..0b111        free
```

## Changes

### runtime/src/fz_value.rs
- Swap TAG_INT / TAG_PTR constants.
- Remove TAG_SPECIAL constant and the SPECIAL_NIL/TRUE/FALSE constants.
- Update `Tag` enum: drop `Special` variant; reorder/relabel.
- Reserve atom ids 0/1/2 at interner setup for nil/true/false.
- `FzValue::NIL`, `FzValue::TRUE`, `FzValue::FALSE` become `from_atom(0/1/2)`.
- Update `from_int`: `((n as u64) << TAG_BITS)` — no OR with tag (tag is 0).
- Update `from_ptr`: `(p as u64) | TAG_PTR`.
- Update `unbox_int`: just `(self.0 as i64) >> TAG_BITS` — no mask check needed because all non-int tags have nonzero bits in the low 3 (so `(self.0 & TAG_MASK) == 0` is the int test).
- Update `unbox_ptr`: `(self.0 & TAG_MASK) == TAG_PTR` → return `(self.0 & !TAG_MASK) as *mut HeapHeader`.
- Update `tag()` match.

### Process / runtime
- Atom interner pre-seeds nil/true/false at indices 0/1/2 at process startup. Existing `atom_names` infrastructure handles this — just populate the first three slots.

### src/ir_codegen.rs
- Integer arithmetic codegen: stop emitting the untag shifts before `iadd`/`isub` when both operands are typed as tagged ints. The arithmetic is now valid directly on tagged representations.
- Pointer deref codegen: emit `band imm -8` (or equivalent) before loads through a Tag::Ptr FzValue.
- Mul/div: still need one shift per the new layout.

### src/typer.rs, src/eval.rs, src/ir_interp.rs
- Drop `Tag::Special` arm; route nil/true/false through the atom path.

### Tests
- Update any test that builds nil/true/false bit patterns directly to use the new constants.
- Add tests asserting:
  - `from_int(0).0 == 0` (zero-initialised memory is integer zero).
  - `from_int(a) + from_int(b)` round-trips correctly when interpreted as raw u64 addition for typed-int values.
  - `from_atom(0) == NIL`, `from_atom(1) == TRUE`, `from_atom(2) == FALSE`.

## Non-changes

- Three-path parity: codegen change is local to ir_codegen; interp and AOT pick up the new representations through fz_value.rs uniformly.
- No impact on GC: tag bits are still the discriminator for "is this a heap pointer," just under a different bit pattern.

## Verification

- All existing tests pass with adjusted constants.
- New tag-arithmetic tests pass.
- Hot-path arithmetic benchmarks show measurable improvement (no untag shifts on int-typed add/sub).
title: fz-tag: tag scheme cleanup — TAG_INT=000 for free arithmetic, drop TAG_SPECIAL
